paper.title: Hypervelocity Stars in the Gaia era. Revisiting the most extreme stars from the MMT survey

extraction.summary: The paper reanalyzes 40 stars from the MMT HVS survey using new spectroscopic models and Gaia DR2 astrometry. 37 stars have full 6D kinematics and are presented as hypervelocity star candidates with bound/unbound probabilities. The extraction includes all 37 kinematic sample stars as candidates possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: MMT HVS survey sample of 42 B-type stars from Brown et al. (2014), restricted to the 40 stars for which MMT spectra are available. Raw spectra are provided for analysis.

source_refs.context: we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample.

method_chain.summary: Multi-band photometry compiled from GALEX, Gaia DR2, SDSS DR12, Pan-STARRS1, SkyMapper DR1.1, BATC, VST-ATLAS-DR3, VST-KiDS-DR3, APASS-DR9, UKIDSS-DR9, and VISTA via VizieR.

source_refs.context: Photometric data were compiled from a variety of surveys covering the ultraviolet, the optical, and the infrared mainly using the VizieR catalog access tool.

method_chain.summary: Proper motions from Gaia DR2 and HST/WFC3+ACS. The more precise measurement is adopted per star: HST for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, HVS13; Gaia DR2 for B711; all others use Gaia DR2. RUWE < 1.4 verified for all.

source_refs.context: Proper motions were mainly taken from Gaia DR2. ... we chose the more precise measurement when Gaia as well as HST proper motions were available, which implies that we used HST data for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, and HVS13 and Gaia DR2 data for B711.

method_chain.summary: From the 40 stars with spectra, 37 have complete astrometric data. HVS11, HVS14, HVS23 lack proper motions and are excluded. This step defines the kinematic sample of 37 stars.

source_refs.context: proper motion measurements were not available for HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23, which is why the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample.

method_chain.summary: Radial velocities obtained from χ² minimization fitting of flux-calibrated MMT spectra against synthetic model spectra. The fit simultaneously solves for radial velocity; other parameters are fitted but their adoption belongs to step-06.

source_refs.context: four fitting parameters for the stellar spectrum: the effective temperature T_eff, the surface gravity log(g), the projected rotational velocity v sin i, and the radial velocity v_rad.

method_chain.summary: T_eff, log g, and v sin i derived from the same spectral fitting procedure as radial velocities. The ADS grid of synthetic spectra (solar composition, 9000-16000 K, log g 3.0-4.8) is used. Results in Table tab:atmos-results.

source_refs.context: four fitting parameters ... T_eff, log(g), v sin i, and v_rad.

method_chain.summary: Spectrophotometric distances from SED fitting. Observed SEDs from compiled photometry are fit with synthetic SEDs (Atlas12 models, fixed T_eff and log g) to obtain angular diameter Θ and E(B-V). Combined with radius from step-08 to compute distance d = 2R/Θ.

source_refs.context: R_V fixed at 3.1, its typical value for the interstellar medium.
The resulting observed SEDs were compared to synthetic ones ... The angular diameters Θ were combined with the stellar radii R to calculate the spectrophotometric distances d.

method_chain.summary: Derivation of stellar masses, ages, radii, luminosities, and Ω/Ω_crit from position in Kiel diagram compared to rotating MS evolutionary tracks (Georgy et al. 2013). Stellar radii feed into distance estimation.

source_refs.context: stellar masses M, ages τ, radii R, luminosities L, and ratios ... Ω/Ω_crit can be derived by comparing the stars' position in the Kiel diagram with theoretical predictions.

method_chain.summary: Solar position and velocity assumptions for Galactocentric coordinate transformation. Paper states R0 = 8.3 kpc, Sun on negative x-axis. Other parameters (z0, v_circ, solar peculiar motion) are adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) / Paper II but not explicitly reported here.

parameters.name: z0
parameters.description: Not explicitly stated in this paper; adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) via Paper II.

parameters.name: v_circ_sun
parameters.description: Not explicitly stated in this paper.

parameters.name: solar_motion_u
parameters.description: Not explicitly stated in this paper.

parameters.name: solar_motion_v
parameters.description: Not explicitly stated in this paper.

parameters.name: solar_motion_w
parameters.description: Not explicitly stated in this paper.

source_refs.context: right-handed Cartesian Galactic coordinate system in which the Sun is located on the negative x-axis and the z-axis points to the Galactic north pole.
The strategy of the kinematic analysis is identical to that of Paper II.

method_chain.summary: Construction of full 6D phase space: positions, proper motions, radial velocities, and distances combined to compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x,y,z) and velocities (vx,vy,vz). Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf = (vx²+vy²+vz²)^(1/2).

source_refs.context: Complementing our revised radial velocities and spectrophotometric distances with positions and proper motions ... gave us the full six-dimensional phase space information.

method_chain.summary: Two Galactic mass models: Model I (revision of Allen & Santillan 1991) and Model II (Wilkinson & Evans 1999 flat rotation curve). Both calibrated with Gaia DR2 constraints. Used to compute local escape velocities and integrate orbits.

source_refs.context: Model~I is a revision of the popular model by Allen & Santillan (1991)
Model~II is based on the flat rotation curve model by Wilkinson & Evans (1999)
The trajectories of all targets were computed in two different Milky Way mass models.

method_chain.summary: Monte Carlo orbit integration with 1.5 million realizations, backwards in time up to 15 Gyr. Correlated proper motion errors from Gaia DR2 included. Plane-crossing events within 99% confidence limit on stellar age are recorded. Flight times and disk-crossing parameters derived.

source_refs.context: Propagation of uncertainties ... standard Monte Carlo approach with 1.5 million realizations.

method_chain.summary: Bound/unbound assessment: local escape velocity v_esc from potential model, compared to v_Grf. Bound probability P_b (%) from fraction of bound Monte Carlo realizations. Values tabulated for both Model I and Model II.

source_refs.context: The Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf, the local Galactic escape velocity v_esc ... are listed.

method_chain.summary: Classification of stars into origin categories: unconstrained, outer rim, disk origin. Ejection velocities v_ej,p computed at plane crossing, correcting for Galactic rotation. Flight times compared to stellar ages.

source_refs.context: we group the stars in three categories based on their inferred spatial origin.
the ejection velocity v_ej,p ... is a more useful quantity.

method_chain.summary: Quality assessment of MMT spectral data: wavelength-averaged S/N, reduced χ² at best fit, number of individual exposures per star. These metrics appear in Table tab:atmos-results as quality indicators. RUWE from Gaia DR2 used for astrometric quality (all <1.4).

source_refs.context: the average S/N of the co-added spectra is only of the order of 10--30.
Table tab:atmos-results listing S/N_tot, χ²_red, and N.
For all objects in the sample, RUWE is below the recommended value of 1.4.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS1 is a known hypervelocity star from the MMT HVS survey, reanalyzed here with new spectroscopic models and Gaia DR2 astrometry. The paper presents kinematic quantities showing it is gravitationally unbound (P_b=0). It is included as an unbound HVS candidate.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The star is explicitly listed in the kinematic tables as a hypervelocity star with bound probability derived, and the paper discusses its unbound status.

source_refs.context: The detailed results of the kinematic analyses are listed in Tables tab:kinematic-AS and tab:kinematic-TF for both Galactic mass models.
we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample.

radial_velocity.description: Statistical uncertainty from line 16. Systematic uncertainty (not included): +1.6/-1.6

Whether a star is bound to the Galaxy or not is determined by the Galactic potential.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B537 is a high-velocity B-type star from the MMT HVS survey, included in the kinematic analysis. Bound probability P_b=100% (Model I). Origin region: disk origin (inner disk).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values are directly taken from paper tables and text.

source_refs.context: This star is the only one that likely originates in the inner disk, that is, inside the solar circle.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B572 is a high-velocity B-type star from the MMT HVS survey, included in the kinematic analysis. Bound probability P_b=100% (Model I). Origin region: disk origin (beyond solar circle).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values are directly taken from paper tables and text.

source_refs.context: B143 and B572 ... The Galactic plane-crossing contours for both stars locate their origin somewhat beyond the solar circle.
B572 ... locate their origin somewhat beyond the solar circle.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B576 is a high-velocity star from the MMT survey. Assuming a main-sequence nature, it is unbound (P_b=0%) and would have an extragalactic origin. The paper also considers a BHB nature for which it would be bound. This record uses the MS parameters.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values taken directly from the kinematic table for the MS case.

source_refs.context: Assuming a MS nature for B576, it would be the only star in the sample for which an origin in the Galactic disk could be ruled out with more than 2σ confidence.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B598 is a high-velocity star from the MMT HVS survey, probably a pre-ELM WD or blue straggler. Assuming a MS nature, it is bound (P_b=100%). Its origin is consistent with the Galactic disk.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values taken directly from the kinematic table for the MS case.

source_refs.context: if it were a MS star, it would be one of our best candidates for the ejection by the Hills mechanism
B598 ... its inferred place of origin, which encloses the GC.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B711 is a B-type runaway star originally classified as bound in the MMT HVS survey. This paper's kinematic analysis finds it bound (P_b=100%), but it is included as part of the 37-star kinematic sample assessed for unbound status.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All quantities clearly tabulated with uncertainties; bound probability unambiguous.

source_refs.context: the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample

For eight stars in the sample, the precision of the kinematic analysis is sufficient to conclude that those stars either stem from the outer rim ... Assuming a MS nature, only three stars (B167, B434, B711) have boundness probabilities larger than 50%
For all objects in the sample, RUWE is below the recommended value of $1.4$, indicating that the astrometric solutions are well-behaved.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B733 is a B-type runaway star from the MMT HVS survey. The kinematic analysis finds it bound (P_b=100%), but it is included as part of the kinematic sample assessed for unbound status.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All quantities clearly tabulated; bound probability unambiguous.

source_refs.context: the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample
B733 is the second closest object ... Combined with the unprecedented astrometric precision of Gaia, it is possible to pinpoint the star's place of origin to a narrow region.
For all objects in the sample, RUWE is below the recommended value of $1.4$
B733 ... it is possible to pinpoint the star's place of origin to a narrow region close but slightly outside of the solar radius
For ten stars in the sample, the precision of the astrometric input data is high enough to conclude that their spatial origin is very likely located within the Galactic disk; see Figs.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B1080 is a B-type star from the MMT HVS survey. Its bound probability is P_b=67% (likely bound), and its spatial origin is unconstrained. The paper does not claim it is unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Values tabulated; bound probability known.

the boundness probability of all stars except B1080 (P_b=67%) is lower than 50%

the origin of 19 stars in the sample is not really constrained ... B1080 (P_b=67%)

inclusion_assessment.summary: B1085 is a B-type star from the MMT HVS survey that this paper finds likely unbound (P_b=21% in Model I, P_b=2% in Model II).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states B1085 would likely be unbound; bound probability is low.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Original MMT HVS survey sample of 42 B-type stars with Galactic rest-frame radial velocity exceeding +275 km/s, selected from Brown et al. (2014).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Of the 42 stars, 40 have MMT spectra analyzed in this paper; 37 have proper motions enabling full kinematic analysis and bound/unbound assessment. Two stars lack spectra; three (HVS11, HVS14, HVS23) lack proper motions.

source_refs.context: we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Stars with full 6D kinematics (positions, proper motions, radial velocities, spectrophotometric distances) for which bound/unbound probabilities, escape velocities, and origin assessments are presented.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these 37 stars as HVS candidates with complete kinematic analysis, providing bound probabilities v_Grf - v_esc, and origin categories. They are the primary candidate set.

source_refs.context: the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample.
The detailed results of the kinematic analyses are listed in Tables tab:kinematic-AS and tab:kinematic-TF.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Supplementary kinematic rows in Model I/II tables for HVS5 using Brown (2015) parameters, B576 under the BHB assumption (M=0.5 M_sun), and B598 under the ELM WD assumption (M=0.25 M_sun). These are alternative distance/mass scenarios for the same stars.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These rows are not independent candidates but different model assumptions for the same objects. The primary MS-based rows are used for the candidate list. The alternatives are discussed in the paper text for comparison.

source_refs.context: HVS5: we illustrate the impact of the different distance estimates on the outcome of the kinematic analysis.

B576: Assuming a MS nature ... it would be the only star in the sample for which an origin in the Galactic disk could be ruled out ... However, the measured rotational velocity ... make it much more plausible that B576 is actually a low-mass BHB star.

B598: a puzzling object ... if B598 was a MS star, it would be one of our best candidates for the ejection by the Hills mechanism ... However, the star is likely an evolved low mass star of ~0.2 M_sun.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Two stars from the original 42-star MMT sample for which MMT spectra were not available to the authors.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Without spectra, spectrophotometric analysis is impossible; these objects cannot be assessed as HVS candidates in this work.

source_refs.context: We did not have access to the spectra of the two missing object.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Stars HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23 which have MMT spectra and derived atmospheric/stellar parameters but lack proper motion measurements from Gaia DR2 or HST.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Without proper motions, the 6D phase space is incomplete; Galactic rest-frame velocities cannot be computed and unbound status cannot be assessed.

source_refs.context: proper motion measurements were not available for HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23, which is why the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample.
